tools.name: parse_document
tools.description:
Parse a local document with LocalParse and return its contents.

Use this instead of writing OCR/pdf-to-text scripts: LocalParse recovers
tables a layout model would miss and arithmetic-checks numeric Totals, so
the output is far more reliable on financial/tabular PDFs, scans, and decks.
Supports PDF, images, PPTX, and more. ``result_type`` is one of ``markdown``
(default), ``text``, ``json``, or ``structured`` (the ingestion contract).

tools.name: parse_url
tools.description:
Download a remote document (e.g. a PDF URL) and parse it with LocalParse.

Same accuracy guarantees as ``parse_document``. ``result_type`` is one of
``markdown`` (default), ``text``, ``json``, or ``structured``.

tools.name: parse_folder
tools.description:
Parse every document in a local folder into a named LocalParse "case".

Ideal for ingesting a docs/ or data-room folder. Each file is persisted under
``case_id`` with a folder-relative path; with ``resume`` (default) a re-run
only parses new/changed files (unchanged ones are skipped by content hash).
Returns a JSON summary: parsed/skipped counts and, per parsed file, its
``job_id``, persisted ``doc_id``, and any arithmetic ``identity_violations``
(a non-zero count means a Total didn't reconcile — worth reviewing).

tools.name: get_structured
tools.description:
Fetch the versioned structured-document contract for a finished job.

Returns the JSON ingestion contract (ordered blocks with provenance:
page, section_path, type, table_markdown, bbox, needs_review, ...).

tools.name: case_manifest
tools.description:
List a case's already-ingested documents (source_path + content hash + status).

Lets the agent see what's in a case before re-ingesting or to plan a delta.

tools.name: case_failures
tools.description: List a case's failed documents (error, attempts, timestamps) for reingest.

tools.name: extract
tools.description:
Extract structured JSON from parsed markdown using a JSON Schema.

Use this *after* parsing a document with ``parse_document``/``parse_url``
when you don't need the full text, only specific fields shaped to a
schema (vendor + total off an invoice, headline numbers off a 10-K, etc.).
The server runs Kimi K2.6 against the schema and **validates the response
server-side** — the JSON you get back is guaranteed to match. Provide an
optional ``system_prompt`` to bias the extraction. Returns the validated
JSON object as a pretty-printed string.

tools.name: extract_document
tools.description:
Parse a local document **and** extract structured JSON in one step.

The agent-friendly end-to-end: give it a file path + a JSON Schema, get
back the validated JSON. Internally this is ``parse_document`` (to
markdown) → ``extract`` (against your schema) — both calls metered
against the same monthly page quota.

findings.title: Parameter 'data_schema' in 'extract' has no type constraint
findings.description: Untyped parameters accept any value, making input validation harder to enforce.
findings.remediation: Add an explicit type to parameter 'data_schema'.

findings.title: Parameter 'system_prompt' in 'extract' has no type constraint
findings.description: Untyped parameters accept any value, making input validation harder to enforce.
findings.remediation: Add an explicit type to parameter 'system_prompt'.

findings.title: Parameter 'data_schema' in 'extract_document' has no type constraint
findings.description: Untyped parameters accept any value, making input validation harder to enforce.
findings.remediation: Add an explicit type to parameter 'data_schema'.

findings.title: Parameter 'system_prompt' in 'extract_document' has no type constraint
findings.description: Untyped parameters accept any value, making input validation harder to enforce.
findings.remediation: Add an explicit type to parameter 'system_prompt'.

findings.title: Token Exposure — credential in response in 'parse_document'
findings.description: Tool response contains credential in response.
findings.evidence: LocalParse error: An API key is required: pass api_key=... or set LOCALPARSE_API_KEY.
findings.remediation: Strip sensitive data from tool responses. Never return raw environment variables, credentials, or internal paths.

findings.title: Token Exposure — credential in response in 'parse_folder'
findings.description: Tool response contains credential in response.
findings.evidence: LocalParse error: An API key is required: pass api_key=... or set LOCALPARSE_API_KEY.
findings.remediation: Strip sensitive data from tool responses. Never return raw environment variables, credentials, or internal paths.

findings.title: Token Exposure — credential in response in 'get_structured'
findings.description: Tool response contains credential in response.
findings.evidence: LocalParse error: An API key is required: pass api_key=... or set LOCALPARSE_API_KEY.
findings.remediation: Strip sensitive data from tool responses. Never return raw environment variables, credentials, or internal paths.

findings.title: 'parse_document.path' silently accepts wrong type
findings.description: Expected type 'string', sent str value '' — no error returned.
findings.remediation: Validate parameter types strictly and return clear JSON-RPC errors on mismatch.

findings.title: 'parse_document' accepts undeclared extra fields
findings.description: Tool accepted arguments containing '__proto__' without error.
findings.remediation: Reject calls containing parameters not declared in inputSchema.

findings.title: Slow tool response — 'parse_url' took 21.1s
findings.description: Tool 'parse_url' took 21.1 seconds to respond. Slow responses can be used for resource exhaustion in agent pipelines or indicate unbounded computation triggered by benign inputs.
findings.remediation: Enforce server-side timeouts on all tool executions. Cap at 30s max.

findings.title: SSRF (timeout) — 'parse_url.url' hung on SSRF payload
findings.description: Call with SSRF payload 'http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/' timed out after 12.0s, suggesting the server is attempting the network request.
findings.remediation: Block outbound requests to RFC-1918 and link-local ranges. Use an allowlist for permitted URL schemes and hosts.

findings.title: SSRF (timing) — 'parse_url.result_type' delayed on SSRF payload
findings.description: Call with payload 'http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/' took 9.1s vs baseline 0.2s — server likely attempted the outbound request before timing out.
findings.remediation: Block outbound requests to RFC-1918 and link-local ranges. Use an allowlist for permitted URL schemes and hosts.

findings.title: initialize accepts protocol version downgrade
findings.description: The server accepted initialize with arbitrary protocol versions: ['9999-99-99', '2030-01-01', '1.0', '2024-01-01', '""', '0.1']. The server also accepted far-future versions, indicating no version validation at all. Servers should reject versions outside their supported range to prevent feature-downgrade attacks.
findings.remediation: Validate protocolVersion against a supported range. Return a JSON-RPC error for versions outside that range.

findings.title: initialize accepts type-confused protocolVersion (integer instead of string)
findings.description: The server accepted an initialize request where protocolVersion was an integer (42) instead of a string. Strict type checking prevents parser confusion and unexpected coercion behavior.
findings.remediation: Validate that protocolVersion is a string before processing.

findings.title: MCP server exposes tools with completable arguments
findings.description: 8 tool(s) have schema properties that may support completion/complete enumeration.
findings.remediation: Review which argument values are exposed via completion/complete.

findings.title: Server hangs on path traversal cursor
findings.description: The server crashed or disconnected when processing an invalid cursor value. This is a Denial of Service vector.
findings.remediation: Validate cursor values before processing; return -32602 (Invalid params) for malformed cursors.

findings.title: Protocol crash — JSON-RPC batch array caused server disconnect
findings.description: Sending a JSON-RPC 2.0 batch (5 requests as an array) caused the server to crash or disconnect. MCP does not define batch semantics; servers must reject arrays gracefully.
findings.evidence: Server disconnected after batch. Pre-crash response: none
findings.remediation: Validate input type at the JSON-RPC layer: if the parsed value is a list, return -32600 (Invalid Request) immediately without further processing.

findings.title: Protocol crash — oversized method name caused transport failure
findings.description: The server failed to respond to a request with an 8192-byte method name. A robust server should return a JSON-RPC error, not disconnect or crash.
findings.remediation: Add input length validation at the JSON-RPC parsing layer. Return -32700 (parse error) or -32600 (invalid request) for malformed inputs.

findings.title: Protocol crash — deeply nested params caused server disconnect
findings.description: Sending a tools/call request with 50-level nested params caused the server to disconnect or crash. Deep nesting can cause stack overflows or excessive memory use in recursive JSON parsers.
findings.remediation: Limit JSON object nesting depth at parse time (recommended: ≤32 levels). Return -32700 (Parse error) for inputs exceeding the depth limit.

findings.title: Protocol crash — string params caused server disconnect
findings.description: Sending a tools/call request where params is a string (not an object) caused the server to crash or disconnect. The JSON-RPC spec requires params to be an array or object.
findings.remediation: Validate params type (must be object or array) before processing. Return -32600 (Invalid Request) for wrong types.

findings.title: Server accepts requests without jsonrpc field
findings.description: The server returned a successful result to a request missing the required 'jsonrpc' field. Per JSON-RPC 2.0, this field is mandatory and must equal '2.0'.
findings.remediation: Validate that the 'jsonrpc' field is present and equals '2.0' before processing. Return -32600 (Invalid Request) for non-conformant messages.

findings.title: Server accepts array request ID — JSON-RPC spec violation
findings.description: The server returned a successful result to a request with an array as the request ID. JSON-RPC 2.0 requires the id to be a string, number, or null.
findings.remediation: Validate that the request 'id' is a string, number, or null. Return -32600 (Invalid Request) for array or object IDs.
